description: This figure presents a paired micrograph and elemental map set from Figure 13, showing cross-sectional morphology and compositional distribution associated with Region IV in the Fe-Mn-Al oxidation map (aluminum >9 wt.%). The left image is an SEM micrograph of an alloy oxidized at 800°C in pure oxygen at 200 torr, revealing a small oxide nodule that has penetrated through the alumina scale; a thin, darker aluminum-rich oxide layer is visible at the nodule-alloy interface. The right image is the corresponding X-ray elemental distribution map, which the paper reports was obtained by electron-probe microanalysis; bright dot clusters are concentrated within the nodule region, and the accompanying text identifies these nodules as being composed of one or two manganese oxide phases, confirming localized manganese enrichment. Together, the images provide direct microstructural and compositional evidence that alloys with aluminum levels exceeding 9% form alumina scales which are locally breached by penetrating manganese-rich oxide nodules, thereby compromising scale protectivity and supporting the paper’s broader conclusion that the most oxidation-resistant compositions lie within the fully ferritic Fe–(5–10)%Mn–(6–10)%Al range.
